---
title: "family support recovery agreement"
format: html
---

## Family Support Recovery Agreement

Clear agreements reduce confusion and conflict in recovery. This worksheet helps define what support looks like, what boundaries are needed, and how to respond to risk moments.

Agreements work best when they are specific, realistic, and revisited regularly.

Support is most effective when it is specific, timely, and easy to access. In high-risk moments, vague plans can lead to delay, while clear roles and contact plans make help more available.

Strengthening support structure reduces isolation and improves accountability. It also helps you recover faster after setbacks by reconnecting you to people and routines that stabilize recovery.

::: callout
### Instructions
Complete this with a family member or support person if possible.
:::

| Topic | Agreement |
|---|---|
| How we communicate when stress rises | |
| What support I want during cravings | |
| What does not help | |
| How we handle high-risk situations | |
| Weekly check-in time | |
